steps.title: Fecal samples should be stored refrigerated...
steps.instruction: Fecal samples should be stored refrigerated at 4 °C until processed.

steps.title: Weigh 8 g of feces from...
steps.instruction: Weigh 8 g of feces from each sample into 50 mL conical tubes and record weights. Note: The amount of feces-PBS slurry is scalable at a 1:4 ratio and as little as 2 g of feces in 8 mL of 1X PBS Life Technologies Catalog #10010-023 can be used.

steps.title: Add 32 mL of 1X PBS...
steps.instruction: Add 32 mL of 1X PBS Life Technologies Catalog #10010-023 to the 8 g of feces in the 50 mL conical tube. The feces in PBS will hereafter be called the feces-PBS slurry. Figure 1. Making the Feces-PBS slurry.

steps.title: Use a sterile, polyester tipped applicator...
steps.instruction: Use a sterile, polyester tipped applicator (swab) to collect a small amount of feces-PBS slurry and inoculate the first quadrant of a mCCDA-CAT plate and a Campy-Cefex agar plate and then streak for isolation. Positive control (ATCC 33291, ATCC 33292, or ATCC 33560 Campylobacter jejuni ) and negative control (ATCC 29522 E. coli ) bacteria may be inoculated onto separate agar plates comparison.

steps.title: Samples should be processed within 72:00:00...
steps.instruction: Samples should be processed within 72:00:00 of receipt at the laboratory.

steps.title: Plates should be incubated in a...
steps.instruction: Plates should be incubated in a microaerophilic environment at 42 °C, + or - 2 .

steps.title: Place plates in a gas-tight canister...
steps.instruction: Place plates in a gas-tight canister with a gas-generating sachet AnaeroPack™-MicroAero Gas Generator Thermo Fisher Catalog #R681005 and place in an incubator at 42 °C, + or - 2 for 48:00:00 . Note the date and time that plates are placed in the incubator.

steps.title: Proceed to Step 20 to examine...
steps.instruction: Proceed to Step 20 to examine mCCDA-CAT and Campy-Cefex plates for suspect colonies.

steps.title: Record the date and time the...
steps.instruction: Record the date and time the plates were removed from the incubator.

steps.title: Examine mCCDA-CAT and Campy-Cefex plates for suspect colonies
steps.instruction: Examine mCCDA-CAT and Campy-Cefex plates for suspect colonies. Colonies have a watery appearance and may have an orangish, tan color on Campy-Cefex agar (Figure 2) or colonies may be white or clear on mCCDA-CAT plates (Figure 3). Figure 2. Campylobacter jejuni on Campy-Cefex agar. Figure 3. Campylobacter jejuni on mCCDA-CAT agar.

steps.title: Isolate up to 4 suspect colonies...
steps.instruction: Isolate up to 4 suspect colonies and subculture suspect colonies onto blood agar plates trypticase soy agar supplemented with 5% sheeps blood plates Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #221261 For identification at the time of isolation, suspect colonies can be done by 1) sub-culturing colonies to blood agar plates for purification, incubating the plates for 48 hrs, and then performing Gram stain and biochemical tests; or 2) directly with matrix-assisted, laser desorption and ionization, time of flight (MALDI-TOF) mass spectrometry according to the manufacturer's protocol; or 3) the C. jejuni gyrA PCR [see related protocols for the PCR process: a) Extraction of DNA from canine feces for detection of Campylobacter jejuni using the MagMax TM CORE Nucleic Acid Purification Kit on KingFisher TM Flex Instrument; b) Extraction of DNA from canine feces for detection of Campylobacter jejuni using MagMax TM CORE Nucleic Acid Purification Kit on KingFisher TM DuoPrime Instrument; or c) Manual DNA Extraction Protocol for Campylobacter Detection in Canine and Bovine Feces]. Colonies from the mCCDA-CAT plate are accurately identified using Bruker Biotyper MALDI-TOF without prior subculture to blood agar plates.

steps.title: Place plates in a gas-tight canister...
steps.instruction: Place plates in a gas-tight canister with a gas-generating sachet AnaeroPack™-MicroAero Gas Generator Thermo Fisher Catalog #R681005 and place in an incubator at 42 °C, + or - 2 for 48:00:00 . Note the date and time that plates are placed in the incubator.

steps.title: Record the date and time the...
steps.instruction: Record the date and time the plates were removed from the incubator.

steps.title: Confirm identification of the colonies as...
steps.instruction: Confirm identification of the colonies as Campylobacter species through MALDI-TOF, PCR, or biochemical testing. Campylobacter jejuni is a Gram-negative curved rod that is catalase positive and oxidase positive. The test for catalase is performed using 3% hydrogen peroxide. Oxidase testing can be performed with oxidase reagent from a variety of suppliers including Oxidase reagent Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #261181 Campylobacter jejuni subsp. doylei has variable catalase activity.

steps.title: After isolation, identification of suspect colonies...
steps.instruction: After isolation, identification of suspect colonies can be done by 1) sub-culturing colonies to blood agar plates for purification, incubating the plates for 48 hrs, and then performing Gram stain and biochemical tests; or 2) directly with matrix-assisted, laser desorption and ionization, time of flight (MALDI-TOF) mass spectrometry according to the manufacturer's protocol; or 3) the C. jejuni gyrA PCR [see related protocols for the PCR process: a) Extraction of DNA from canine feces for detection of Campylobacter jejuni using the MagMax TM CORE Nucleic Acid Purification Kit on KingFisher TM Flex Instrument; b) Extraction of DNA from canine feces for detection of Campylobacter jejuni using MagMax TM CORE Nucleic Acid Purification Kit on KingFisher TM DuoPrime Instrument; or c) Manual DNA Extraction Protocol for Campylobacter Detection in Canine and Bovine Feces].

steps.title: Wipe down sample tubes and gloves...
steps.instruction: Wipe down sample tubes and gloves with 10% bleach to minimize cross-contamination.
